steps.title: Place the example in context
steps.content: The passage first explains that ordinary soap bubbles pop because of drainage or evaporation. It then says Moreau and colleagues found ways to mitigate those factors, allowing bubbles to last much longer.

steps.title: Reject reversed or invented roles
steps.content: Choice B points to circumstances that prompted the research, but the underlined portion comes after the discovery as an example of a method. Choice C reverses the logic by suggesting the unwanted evaporation must inevitably occur. Choice D invents future testing, which the text does not mention.

steps.title: Connect glycerol to durability
steps.content: The underlined sentence is not merely naming an ingredient. It explains a mechanism: glycerol adheres to water molecules, so the bubble can replace water lost to evaporation and avoid one cause of popping.

steps.title: Describe the process effect
steps.content: The underlined portion explains that glycerol in the bubble shell draws in water molecules from the surrounding air, compensating for evaporation. That describes how the researchers’ process increases the longevity of the bubble. The answer is A.
